Strings.title: Valid Palindrome
Strings.description:
A phrase is a palindrome if, after converting all uppercase letters into lowercase letters and removing all non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward.

Given a string `s`, return `True` if it is a palindrome, or `False` otherwise.

examples.input: s = 'A man, a plan, a canal: Panama'

constraints: s consists only of printable ASCII characters

input.s: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama
Was it a car or a cat I saw?

hints: Try using two pointers
Filter out non-alphanumeric chars first

Strings.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Strings.description: Given a string `s`, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
constraints: s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces
hints: Consider using a sliding window
A set or dict can track seen characters

Strings.title: Anagram Check
Strings.description:
Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `True` if `t` is an anagram of `s`, and `False` otherwise.

An anagram uses the same characters the same number of times, in any order.
constraints: s and t consist of lowercase English letters
Strings.function_signature: def is_anagram(s: str, t: str) -> bool:
hints: Sorting both strings is one approach
A frequency counter (dict/Counter) is O(n)

Strings.title: String Reversal Mastery
Strings.description:
Implement a function that reverses a string **without** using Python's built-in `[::-1]` slice or `reversed()`. You must use explicit iteration or recursion.

Return the reversed string.
constraints: Do not use s[::-1] or reversed()
hints: Try using a while loop with two pointers
Or build up a result string character by character

Lists.title: List Comprehension and In-Place Mutations
Lists.description:
Given a list of integers `nums`, return a **new list** containing only the even numbers, each multiplied by 2.

You must use a **list comprehension** (not a for loop appending to a list).
hints: Syntax: [expr for item in iterable if condition]
Filter with `if num % 2 == 0`

Lists.title: Two Sum
Lists.description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return the **indices** of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice. Return the answer in any order.
Lists.function_signature: def two_sum(nums: list, target: int) -> list:
hints: A hash map lets you find complements in O(1)
For each num, check if (target - num) is already in your map

Lists.title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's Algorithm)
Lists.description:
Given an integer array `nums`, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum.

A subarray is a contiguous part of an array.
hints: Kadane's: track current_sum and max_sum
Reset current_sum to 0 when it goes negative

Lists.title: Rotate Array
Lists.description: Given an integer array `nums`, rotate the array to the right by `k` steps, where `k` is non-negative. Perform rotation **in-place** and return the modified list.
Lists.function_signature: def rotate(nums: list, k: int) -> list:
hints: k %= len(nums) handles k > len(nums)
The reversal trick: reverse all, then reverse first k, then rest

Dictionaries.title: Group Anagrams
Dictionaries.description:
Given an array of strings `strs`, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in **any order**.

An anagram is a word formed by rearranging letters of another word using all original letters exactly once.
constraints: strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters
hints: Sorted string of an anagram is always the same
Use sorted(word) as the dict key

Dictionaries.title: Top K Frequent Elements
Dictionaries.description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return the `k` most frequent elements. You may return the answer in **any order**.
constraints: k is in the range [1, number of unique elements]
The answer is guaranteed to be unique
Dictionaries.function_signature: def top_k_frequent(nums: list, k: int) -> list:
hints: Count frequencies with a dict or Counter
Sort by frequency or use a heap

Dictionaries.title: Dict Comprehension Mastery
Dictionaries.description:
Given a list of words, return a dictionary where each key is a word and each value is the length of that word.

You must use a **dict comprehension** (not a for loop with assignment).
hints: Syntax: {key_expr: val_expr for item in iterable}
Use len(word) as the value

Arrays.title: Contains Duplicate
Arrays.description: Given an integer array `nums`, return `True` if any value appears **at least twice** in the array, and return `False` if every element is distinct.
hints: A set only stores unique elements
Compare len(nums) vs len(set(nums))

Arrays.title: Product of Array Except Self
Arrays.description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return an array `answer` such that `answer[i]` is equal to the product of all the elements of `nums` except `nums[i]`.

You must solve it in **O(n)** time and **without using division**.
hints: Use a prefix product array and a suffix product array
Then combine: answer[i] = prefix[i] * suffix[i]

Arrays.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
Arrays.description:
You are given an array `prices` where `prices[i]` is the price of a given stock on the `i`th day.

You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a **different day in the future** to sell that stock. Return the maximum profit. If no profit is achievable, return `0`.
hints: Track the minimum price seen so far
At each step, compute profit = current - min_seen

Arrays.title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
Arrays.description:
Suppose an array of length `n` sorted in ascending order is **rotated** between 1 and n times.

Given the sorted rotated array `nums` of unique elements, return the minimum element. You must write an algorithm that runs in **O(log n)** time.
hints: Binary search: compare mid with right pointer
If nums[mid] > nums[right], minimum is on the right half
